rulings.Case Title: BETTY UTTERBACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Monterey Mechanical Co.¿S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories From Plaintiff Russell Utterback
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Monterey Mechanical Co.¿s motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories from plaintiff Russell Utterback is taken off calendar. Plaintiffs¿ opposition memorandum states that plaintiffs served supplemental responses on April 7. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY UTTERBACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Monterey Mechanical Co.¿S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents From Plaintiff Russell Utterback
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Monterey Mechanical Co.¿s motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents from plaintiff Russell Utterback Utterback is granted as to interrogatories 36-53 and held in abeyance as to the remaining interrogatories. Interrogatories 36-53 seek relevant information and are not unduly burdensome so that plaintiffs should be required to respond to them notwithstanding the general rule limiting a party to serving 35 specially-prepared interrogatories. As to the remaining interrogatories in dispute, plaintiffs¿ opposition memorandum states that plaintiffs served supplemental responses on April 7. After meet and confer efforts, if defendant believes that the amended responses are still inadequate, no later than 4:30pm today defendant should send an email to contestasbetostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, which interrogatories it still seeks further responses and then both sides must appear at the hearing on April 13. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Defendant Svi Corporation'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the depositions of defendant SVI Corporation¿s PMK and COR and production of documents is denied. Plaintiffs fail to show that: 1) the deposition transcript of Morgan Campbell is insufficient to satisfy plaintiffs¿ need for the information from defendant and 3) defendant¿s representation that it does not have any responsive documents in its possession, custody or control is untrue. The sanctions request by both plaintiffs and defendant are denied. Neither side acted without substantial justification. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GILBERT FORBES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Re-Open Discovery For The Purpose Of Taking The Deposition Of Dr. Irwin Stoloff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Plaintiff Gilbert Forbes¿s motion to re-open discovery to take the deposition of Dr. Irwin Stoloff: hearing required. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD REENTS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿s motion to compel further responses to discovery is granted. Plaintiffs¿ responses violate the requirements that the responses be ¿straightforward¿ and ¿not evasive As to RFAs 1 and 2, if, as it appears, plaintiffs contend that they lack sufficient information to admit or deny that Mr. Reents was exposed to amphibole fibers from thermal insulation, plaintiffs should so state without qualification and meritless objections. As to RFAs 3 and 4, if, as it appears, plaintiffs believe that Mr. Reents inhaled amphibole fibers and such inhalation was a substantial factor in causing his mesothelioma, plaintiffs should unequivocally so admit without qualification and meritless objections. As to RFA 13, if, as it appears, plaintiffs believe that Mr. Reents was exposed to asbestos-containing products for which defendant is not liable, plaintiffs should unequivocally so admit without qualification and meritless objections. As to RFA 31, if, as it appears, plaintiffs believe that asbestos fibers from products attributable to Johns Manville were a substantial factor in causing Mr. Reents¿ mesothelioma, plaintiffs should unequivocally so admit without qualification and meritless objections. Plaintiffs¿ purported ¿qualifications¿ in their responses are much more in the nature of obfuscations than proper qualifications. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD REENTS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Otis Elevator Co.¿S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 13, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Otis Elevator Co.¿s motion to compel further responses to discovery is granted. Plaintiffs¿ responses violate the requirements that the responses be ¿straightforward¿ and ¿not evasive.¿ As to RFA 11, if, as it appears, plaintiffs believe that Mr. Reents was exposed to asbestos-containing products for which defendant is not liable, plaintiffs should unequivocally so admit without qualification and meritless objections. As to RFAs 13 and 14, if, as it appears, plaintiffs contend that they lack sufficient information to admit or deny that Mr. Reents was exposed to amphibole fibers from thermal insulation, plaintiffs should so state without qualification and meritless objections. As to RFAs 15 and 16, if, as it appears, plaintiffs believe that Mr. Reents inhaled amphibole fibers and such inhalation was a substantial factor in causing his mesothelioma, plaintiffs should unequivocally so admit without qualification and meritless objections. As to RFA 34, if, as it appears, plaintiffs believe that asbestos fibers from products attributable to Johns Manville were a substantial factor in causing Mr. Reents¿ mesothelioma, plaintiffs should unequivocally so admit without qualification and meritless objections. Plaintiffs¿ purported ¿qualifications¿ in their responses are much more in the nature of obfuscations than proper qualifications. = (220/HEK)
